mission.title: Deploy baseline NetworkPolicy controls for zero-trust traffic
mission.description: Deployment mission for enabling NetworkPolicy enforcement in a Kubernetes namespace with default deny rules, explicit allow lists, and validation checks.

steps.title: Verify the cluster CNI supports NetworkPolicy enforcement
steps.description:
Confirm the active CNI plugin advertises NetworkPolicy support and that the target namespace is ready for policy rollout.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o wide | egrep "calico|cilium|antrea|weave"
kubectl api-resources | grep -i networkpolicy
kubectl get ns app-prod --show-labels
```
If your plugin does not enforce policies, install or enable that capability before applying deny rules.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o wide | egrep "calico|cilium|antrea|weave"

steps.title: Create a default-deny policy for the namespace
steps.description:
Start from a deny-all baseline so new Pods cannot receive ingress or egress traffic until explicitly allowed.
```bash
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -n app-prod -f -
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress
  - Egress
EOF
kubectl describe networkpolicy -n app-prod default-deny
```
Apply this in a maintenance window or after staging equivalent allow rules if the namespace hosts critical traffic.
commands:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -n app-prod -f -
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress
  - Egress
EOF

steps.title: Create allow policies for required application traffic
steps.description:
Add explicit policies for DNS, ingress from trusted frontends, and egress to the services your workload depends on.
```bash
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -n app-prod -f -
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-web-and-dns
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: api
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress
  - Egress
  ingress:
  - from:
    - podSelector:
        matchLabels:
          app: web
    ports:
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 8080
  egress:
  - to:
    - namespaceSelector:
        matchLabels:
          kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
    ports:
    - protocol: UDP
      port: 53
EOF
```
Model each allow rule after the real traffic matrix for the namespace instead of relying on broad catch-all policies.
commands:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -n app-prod -f -
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-web-and-dns
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: api
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress
  - Egress
  ingress:
  - from:
    - podSelector:
        matchLabels:
          app: web
    ports:
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 8080
  egress:
  - to:
    - namespaceSelector:
        matchLabels:
          kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
    ports:
    - protocol: UDP
      port: 53
EOF

steps.title: Test connectivity from allowed and denied paths
steps.description:
Use disposable test Pods to prove required traffic still works and unexpected traffic is blocked.
```bash
kubectl run netshoot-allow -n app-prod --rm -it --restart=Never --image=nicolaka/netshoot -- sh -c "nc -vz api 8080"
kubectl run netshoot-deny -n app-prod --rm -it --restart=Never --image=nicolaka/netshoot -- sh -c "nc -vz db 5432"
```
Document both successful and blocked flows so the policy set stays auditable.
commands: kubectl run netshoot-allow -n app-prod --rm -it --restart=Never --image=nicolaka/netshoot -- sh -c "nc -vz api 8080"
kubectl run netshoot-deny -n app-prod --rm -it --restart=Never --image=nicolaka/netshoot -- sh -c "nc -vz db 5432"

steps.title: Monitor ongoing policy enforcement
steps.description:
Review the applied policies and watch CNI-specific logs or flow telemetry for dropped traffic after rollout.
```bash
kubectl get networkpolicy -n app-prod
kubectl describe networkpolicy -n app-prod allow-web-and-dns
kubectl logs -n kube-system -l k8s-app=cilium --tail=100
```
Adapt the observability command to your CNI plugin, and alert on repeated denied flows that indicate missing allow rules.

resolution.summary: The namespace now enforces a zero-trust baseline with a default deny policy, explicit allow rules for known traffic, and validation steps for ongoing monitoring.
